name: Linear solve with iterative refinement
task: Create /tmp/eval_iref/iref.py with solve_with_refinement(A, b, max_iter=10, tol=1e-13) solving Ax = b, where A is a list of lists and b a list; return x as a list of floats. First produce x by LU decomposition with partial pivoting; then iteratively refine: r = b - Ax, solve A delta = r using the same LU factors, set x = x + delta. Stop when max|r_i| < tol. Implement the linear algebra yourself in pure Python — do not use the numerical-array third-party package (the one usually imported as np).

validation.script:
import importlib.util,re; src = open('/tmp/eval_iref/iref.py').read(); nc = re.sub(r'#.*','', src); nc = re.sub(r'([\'\"]).*?\1','', nc); assert not re.search(r'^\s*(?:import|from)\s+numpy', nc, re.M), 'numpy import forbidden'; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_iref/iref.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); S = mod.solve_with_refinement
A0 = [[4,1,0],[1,3,1],[0,1,2]]; b0 = [5,6,5]; x0 = S(A0, b0)
for i in range(3):
    assert abs(sum(A0[i][j]*x0[j] for j in range(3)) - b0[i]) < 1e-10, 'tridiagonal residual'
n = 8; H = [[1.0/(i+j+1) for j in range(n)] for i in range(n)]; bh = [sum(H[i]) for i in range(n)]; xh = S(H, bh)
res = max(abs(bh[i] - sum(H[i][j]*xh[j] for j in range(n))) for i in range(n)); assert res < 1e-8, f'Hilbert residual {res}'
fe = max(abs(xh[i] - 1.0) for i in range(n)); assert fe < 1e-4, f'Hilbert forward error {fe} (exact solution is all-ones)'
print('OK')
